description: Confounder: The model assumes the weight remains float32 and thus the output stays float32 a

_meta.explanation: The rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because no cast back to input dtype occurs after the weight multiplication.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes the weight remains float32 and thus the output stays float32 after multiplication. However, the weight was explicitly cast to bfloat16, so the forward pass casts the normalized x to bfloat16 before multiplying, resulting in bfloat16 output.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because no cast back to input dtype occurs after the weight multiplication.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
